prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design solubilised monomeric variants of the VEGF-A165 cystine-knot core domain (Homo sapiens, PDB 1VPP) for bacterial E. coli expression, removing the obligate dimerisation interface while preserving VEGFR-2 binding activity for structural biology studies. outline a computational approach covering: (1) identification and masking of dimer-interface cysteines (Cys51, Cys60), (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.2) with hydrophilicity-rewarding constraints on the former dimer interface, (3) CamSol and Aggrescan3D scoring to rank solubility predictions, (4) experimental validation by E. coli small-scale expression and size-exclusion chromatography to confirm monomer state. Discuss balance between solubility enhancement and receptor binding affinity retention.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (1VPP); target: VEGF-A165; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: vascular endothelial growth factor A (dimeric cystine-knot growth factor)
